title: irenea fracta
parts: Irenea fracta has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which are utilized in traditional medicine for their therapeutic properties. The roots of the Irenea fracta plant are rich in iridoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while the leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The flowers of this plant are a good source of sesquiterpenes, which have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the aerial parts of the Irenea fracta plant are also used in herbal medicine, containing coumarins and alkaloids that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Irenea fracta can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to liver damage and kidney failure, resulting in symptoms like dark urine, fatigue, and abdominal pain. Improper use of the plant can also cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as exacerbate existing conditions such as epilepsy and heart disease. In extreme cases, the plant's toxins can even trigger life-threatening conditions like anaphylaxis and cardiac arrest.
